title: RNA based library preparation for high through-put full-length small ribosomal RNA sequencing on the Illumina MiSeq and HiSeq platforms
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7vigo","text":"This protocol describes the preparation of Illumina libraries for high through-put sequencing of full-length SSU rRNA sequences from all domains of life without primer bias.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"23pse","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bvfgm","text":"Sequences for primers and adaptor used can be found under guidelines.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

name","sequence","Purification"],["SSU_rRNA_RT1","AGGGGGGCAAAGATGAAGATNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNTCGCCTTAACTTGCCTGTCGCTCTATCTTCTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_RT2","AGGGGGGCAAAGATGAAGATNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNCTAGTACGACTTGCCTGTCGCTCTATCTTCTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_RT3","AGGGGGGCAAAGATGAAGATNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNTTCTGCCTACTTGCCTGTCGCTCTATCTTCTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_RT4","AGGGGGGCAAAGATGAAGATNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNGCTCAGGAACTTGCCTGTCGCTCTATCTTCTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_RT5","AGGGGGGCAAAGATGAAGATNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNAGGAGTCCACTTGCCTGTCGCTCTATCTTCTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_RT6","AGGGGGGCAAAGATGAAGATNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNCATGCCTAACTTGCCTGTCGCTCTATCTTCTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_RT7","AGGGGGGCAAAGATGAAGATNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNGTAGAGAGACTTGCCTGTCGCTCTATCTTCTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_RT8","AGGGGGGCAAAGATGAAGATNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNCCTCTCTGACTTGCCTGTCGCTCTATCTTCTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_RT9","AGGGGGGCAAAGATGAAGATNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNAGCGTAGCACTTGCCTGTCGCTCTATCTTCTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_RT10","AGGGGGGCAAAGATGAAGATNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNCAGCCTCGACTTGCCTGTCGCTCTATCTTCTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_s\u0026nbsp;","/5Phos/GGGCAATATCAGCACCAACAGAAA/3SpC3/","HPLC"],["SSU_rRNA_l","CTCCACCCAGACTCATCCATNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNATAGAGGCTTTCTGTTGGTGCTGATATTGC","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_pcr_rv\u0026nbsp;","AGGGGGGCAAAGATGAAGAT","HPLC"],["SSU_rRNA_pcr_fw","CTCCACCCAGACTCATCCAT","HPLC"],["SSU_rRNA_readtag_rv\u0026nbsp;","CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGATGTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCTAGGGGGGCAAAGATGAAGAT","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_readtag_fw","CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGATGTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCTCTCCACCCAGACTCATCCAT","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_linktag_rv\u0026nbsp;","CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGATCGGTCTCGGCATTCCTGCTGAACCGCTCTTCCGATCTGCAATATCAGCACCAACAGAAA","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_linktag_fw","AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCTGAAGATAGAGCGACAGGCAAGT","PAGE"],["SSU_rRNA_read2_rv","GCTCTTCCGATCTAGGGGGGCAAAGATGAAGAT\u0026nbsp;","HPLC"],["SSU_rRNA_read2_fw","GCTCTTCCGATCTCTCCACCCAGACTCATCCAT\u0026nbsp;","HPLC"]],"guid":"A6BAD2B0458111E992E69DD12CEC957F","legend":"","mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[],"show_headers":true}}}}
steps: Dilute the RNA to 40 ng/μL with nuclease-free water and transfer 25 μL to a 200 μL PCR tube. If the concentration in your sample is less then 40 ng/μL you can concentrate the sample using RNAclean XP beads. Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 1.0x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 25 μL nuclease-free water
Heat denature the RNA at 70°C for 5 min. Snap cool the sample on an ice block for 2 min to prevent RNA from refolding. Immediately proceed to the next step.

Isolated the SSU rRNA from total RNA by size selection on an E-gel electrophoresis system with a precast E-Gel CloneWell gel (Thermo Fisher Scientific): Use 20 μL of the heat denatured RNA (800 ng total RNA) Use 500 ng GeneRuler 1 kB DNA ladder (Thermo Fisher Scientific) as a reference. Run the gel until the SSU peak (ca. 1,500 bp) is approximately 1 mm from the the elution well. Now collect 20 µL elution aliquots every 15 seconds, up to a total of 16 aliquots, and the visible passing of the SSU rRNA peak. Pool every two aliquots to obtain 8 pooled aliquots per sample.
Analyze the pooled aliquots on an Agilent 2200 Tapestation with the High Sensitivity RNA Screen tape and pool the aliquots that contains the SSU rRNA.
Purify the sample using RNAClean XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 1.0x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 32 μL nuclease-free water

Prepare the poly(A) tailing reaction in a 200 µL PCR tube: 30 µL sample from above (10 – 200 ng SSU rRNA) 4 µL 10X E. coli Poly(A) Polymerase Reaction Buffer (New England Biolabs) 4 µL 10 mM ATP (New England Biolabs ) 1.5 µL E. coli Poly(A) Polymerase (New England Biolabs ) 1 µL RiboLock RNase Inhibitor (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Purify the sample using RNAClean XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 1.0x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 15 μL nuclease-free water
Validate the polyadenylation on an Agilent 2200 Tapestation using RNA Screentape. Use the untreated RNA from step 5 as a reference.

Dilute the sample to 2 ng/µL with nuclease-free water based on the gel electrophoresis results. If the concentration of the sample is below 2 ng/µL use it as it is. We have prepared successful libraries with as little as 0.45 ng/μL.
Prepare the adaptor priming reaction in a 200 µL PCR tube: 11.5 µL sample from above (5 - 23 ng polyadenylated SSU rRNA) 0.5 µL 100 µM SSU_rRNA_RT 1 µL 10 mM dNTP mix

Prepare the first strand cDNA synthesis reaction in a 200 μL PCR tube: 13 μL sample from above 4 µL 5x SSIV buffer (Thermo Fisher Scientific) 1 µL 200 U/µL SuperScript IV Reverse Transcriptase (Thermo Fisher Scientific) 1 µL 100 mM DTT (Thermo Fisher Scientific) 1 µL RiboLock RNase Inhibitor (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Perform cDNA synthesis at 50°C for 50 min, inactivate the reaction at 80°C for 10 min, and finally cool the sample to 37°C. Proceed immediately to the next step.
Add 1 µL 10 U/μL Ribonuclease H, from E. coli (cloned) (Thermo Fisher Scientific) to the cDNA from above, and incubate the sample at 37°C for 20 min to remove the RNA template.
Purify the sample using AMPure XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 1.0x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 11 μL nuclease-free water

Prepare the single stranded adapter ligation reaction in a 200 μL PCR tube: 8.25 µL sample from above 2.5 µL 10 µM SSU_rRNA_s 5 µL 10x custom T4 RNA ligase buffer (500 mM Tris-HCl, 100 mM MgCl 2 , 10 mM hexamminecobalt(III) chloride, 200 µM ATP, pH 8.0) 31.25 µL 40% (wt/wt) PEG 8000 (Thermo Fisher Scientific) 0.5 µL 1 mg/mL BSA (Thermo Fisher Scientific) 2.5 µL 10 U/µL T4 RNA ligase (Thermo Fisher Scientific) Recipy for 10x custom T4 RNA ligase buffer: 500 mM Tris-HCl, 100 mM MgCl 2 , 10 mM hexamminecobalt(III) chloride, 200 µM ATP, pH 8.0
Perform ligation at 22°C overnight.

Purify the sample using AMPure XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 1.0x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 50 μL nuclease-free water Repeat the purification with 1.0x beads and elude in 12 μL nuclease-free water High amounts of PEG-8000 in the samples prevents stringent size selection during the AMPure XP purification. A two step AMPure purification is therefore performed to ensure the removal of free adaptor oligoes.
Purify the sample using AMPure XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 0.6x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 25 μL nuclease-free water
Incubate the sample at 70°C for 5 min. Snap cooled on an ice block for 2 min. Proceed immediately to the next step.
Perform polyadenylation at 37°C for 30 min.

Prepare the second strand cDNA synthesis reaction in a 200 μL PCR tube: 10 µL sample from above 26.75 µL nuclease free water 5 µL 10x PCR Buffer (Qiagen) 1 µL 10 mM dNTP (Qiagen) 2.5 µL 10 µM SSU_rRNA_l 2.5 µL 10 µM SSU_rRNA_pcr_rv 2 µL 25 mM MgCl 2 0.25 µL 5 U/µL Taq polymerase (Qiagen)

Perform the second strand cDNA syntheis with an initial denaturation at 94°C for 3 min, followed by 2 cycles of denaturation at 94°C for 30 s, annealing at 56°C for 30 s, and extension at 72°C for 3 min.

Prepare a single-standed DNA digestion reaction in a 200 μL PCR tube: 23 µL sample from above 3 µL 10X S1 nuclease buffer (Thermo Fisher Scientific) 3 µL 3 M NaCl (Thermo Fisher Scientific) 1 µL 10 U/µL S1 nuclease (Thermo Fisher Scientific) This reaction removes the remaining single-stranded cDNA, which would otherwise hamper the following PCR.
Perform digestion at at 25 °C for 25 min. Terminate the reaction by addition of 2 µL 0.5 M EDTA and heating to 70°C for 10 min.

Purify the sample using AMPure XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Dilute the sample to 50 μL with nuclease-free water (18 μL) Use 0.6x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 12 μL nuclease-free water

Prepare a library amplification reaction in a 200 μL PCR tube: 10 µL sample from above 63.5 µL nuclease-free water 10 µL 10x PCR buffer (Qiagen) 2 µL 10 mM dNTP (Qiagen) 5 µL 10 µM fSSU_rRNA_pcr_fw 5 µL 10 µM fSSU_rRNA_pcr_rv 4 µL 25 mM MgCl (Qiagen) 0.5 µL 5 U/µL Taq polymerase (Qiagen) The ds cDNA is amplified by PCR to obtain enough product for validation, size selection, and quantification.

Perform the PCR with an initial denaturation at 94°C for 3 min, followed by 20 cycles of denaturation at 94°C for 30 s, annealing at 62°C for 30 s, and extension at 72°C for 2 min and then a final extension at 72°C for 5 min.
Purify the sample using AMPure XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 0.6x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 20 μL nuclease-free water
Analyze the PCR product on an Agilent 2200 Tapestation using a D5000 screen tape.

Isolated the full-length SSU PCR products by size selection on an E-gel electrophoresis system with precast E-Gel CloneWell gels (Thermo Fisher Scientific): Use 20 μL of sample from above (100-300 ng of PCR product) Use 500 ng GeneRuler 1 kB DNA ladder (Thermo Fisher Scientific) as a reference. Run the gel until the SSU peak (ca. 1,500 bp) is approximately 1 mm from the the elution well. Now collect 20 µL elution aliquots every 15 seconds, up to a total of 16 aliquots, and the visible passing of the full-length SSU cDNA peak. Pool every two aliquots to obtain 8 pooled aliquots per sample.
Analyze the pooled aliquots on an Agilent 2200 Tapestation with the High Sensitivity D5000 Screentape and pool the aliquots that contains the full-length SSU cDNA peak.
Purify the sample using AMPure XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 0.6x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 15 μL nuclease-free water
Determine the quality and the concentration of the final library on an Agilent 2200 Tapestation with the High Sensitivity D5000 Screentape.

Dilute the primary libraries based on the tapestation data to 10,000 (MiSeq) or 100,000 (HiSeq) copies/µL with nuclease-free water. The number of amplicons in the primary library can be calculated using the following formula: number = ( amount in ng * 6.022x10 23 ) / ( length in bp * 1x10 9 * 650 ) => number = ( amount in ng * 6.022x10 23 )/ ( 1800 * 1x10 9 * 650 ) => number = amount in ng * 5.15x10 8 copies/ng

Prepare a clonal library amplification reaction in a 200 μL PCR tube: 10 µL sample from above 63.5 µL nuclease-free water 10 µL 10x PCR buffer (Qiagen) 2 µL 10 mM dNTP (Qiagen) 5 µL 10 µM SSU_rRNA_pcr_fw 5 µL 10 µM SSU_rRNA_pcr_rv 4 µL 25 mM MgCl (Qiagen) 0.5 µL 5 U/µL Taq polymerase (Qiagen)

Perform the PCR with an initial denaturation at 94°C for 3 min, followed by 25 (MiSeq) or 20 (HiSeq) cycles of denaturation at 94°C for 30 s, annealing at 62°C for 30 s, and extension at 72°C for 2 min and then a final extension at 72°C for 5 min.
Purify the sample using AMPure XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 0.6x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 20 μL nuclease-free water
Determine the quality of the clonal library on an Agilent 2200 Tapestation with the D5000 Screentape.
Determine the concentration of the clonal library on a Qubit 2.0 fluorometer with the Qubit dsDNA HS Assay kit. Use 1 μL sample Perform duplicate measurements
Dilute the clonal library to 4.35 ng/μL with nuclease-free water

A Nextera library preparation kit (Illumina) is used to prepare a paired-end read-tag sequencing library from the clonal library using a customized protocol. Prepare a tagmentation reaction in a 200 μL PCR tube: 23 μL diluted clonal library from above 12.5 µL tagment DNA buffer (Illumina) 1.5 µL tagment DNA enzyme (Illumina)
Perform the tagmentation incubated at 55°C for 5 min. Immediately proceed to the next step.

Purify the sample using AMPure XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Dilute the sample to 100 μL with nuclease-free water (63 μL) Use 0.6x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 42 μL nuclease-free water

The tagmentation products are PCR amplified using two separate PCRs (A and B). PCR A selectively amplified fragments containing the 5’ termini of the cDNA amplicons and PCR B selectively amplified fragments containing the 3’ termini. Prepare the PCR reactions in 200 μL PCR tubes: 20 μL of sample from above 5 µL N504 nextera adaptor (Illumina) 5 µL 10 µM SSU_rRNA_readtag_fw (PCR A) or SSU_rRNA_readtag_rv (PCR B) 15 µL Nextera PCR master mix (Illumina) 5 µL PCR primer cocktail (Illumina)

Perform the PCR with an initial elongation at 72°C for 3 min, denaturation at 98°C for 3 s, followed by 10 cycles of denaturation at 98°C for 10 s, annealing at 60°C for 30 s, and extension at 72°C for 3 min and then a final extension at 72°C for 5 min.
Purify the sample using AMPure XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 1.0x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 20 μL nuclease-free water

Size selection the read-tag libraries on an E-gel electrophoresis system with precast E-Gel CloneWell gels (Thermo Fisher Scientific): Use 20 μL of sample from above Use 500 ng GeneRuler 1 kB DNA ladder (Thermo Fisher Scientific) as a reference. Run the gel until the 500 bp marker is approximately 1 mm from the the elution well. Now collect 20 µL elution aliquots every 15 seconds, up to a total of 32 aliquots, and the visible passing of the 1500 bp marker. Pool every two aliquots to obtain 16 pooled aliquots per sample. This size selection is perform in order to normalize the read-tag libraries with respect to sequencing efficiency. This results in even coverage of the full-length SSU molecules during the assembly.
Determine the size and the concentration of each fraction on an Agilent 2200 Tapestation with the High Sensitivity D1000 Screentape.

Calculate the effective sequencing concentration for each fraction based on the tapestation data and the empirical formula: C seq = Peak molarity [pmol/l] * (-0.0124*(peak size [bp] – 215 bp) + 10.332).

Pool equal sequencing capacity of each fractions with an average size of 550-1200 bp. We use the whole sample with an average size of approximately 1000 bp. The amounts of the other fractions are determined based on the sequencing concentration and volumen of this sample.
Purify the sample using AMPure XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 1.0x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 12 μL nuclease-free water
Validate the final read-tag libraries on an Agilent 2200 Tapestation with the High Sensitivity D1000 Screentape.
Determine the concentration of the read-tag libraries on a Qubit 2.0 fluorometer with the Qubit dsDNA HS Assay kit. Use 1 μL sample Perform duplicate measurements

Prepare a clonal library end-repair reaction in a 200 μL PCR tube: 4.6 µL diluted clonal library from step 40 16.65 µL nuclease-free water 2.5 µL 10X NEBNext End repair Reaction Buffer (New England Biolabs) 1.25 µL NEBNext End Repair Enzyme Mix (New England Biolabs)
Perform end-repair at 20°C for 30 min.
Purify the sample using AMPure XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 1.0x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 10 μL nuclease-free water

Prepare a intramolecular blunt-end ligation reaction in a 200 μL PCR tube: 2 µL sample from above 150 µL nuclease-free water 20 µL 50% (w/w) PEG 4000 solution (New England Biolabs) 20 µL 10X T4 DNA ligase buffer (New England Biolabs) 8 µL T4 DNA ligase (New England Biolabs)
Perform ligation at 16°C for 1 hr.
Purify the sample using AMPure XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 1.0x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 10 μL nuclease-free water

Prepare a junction amplification reaction in a 200 μL PCR tube: 8 µL sample from above 5 µL 10x PCR buffer (Qiagen) 1 µL 10 mM dNTP (Qiagen) 2.5 µL 10 µM SSU_rRNA_linktag_fw 2.5 µL 10 µM SSU_rRNA_linktag_rv 3µL 25 mM MgCl (Qiagen) 30.25 µL nuclease-free water 0.25 µL 5 U/µL Taq polymerase (Qiagen)

Perform the PCR with an initial denaturation at 94°C for 3 min, followed by 20 cycles of denaturation at 94°C for 20 s, annealing at 56°C for 20 s, and extension at 72°C for 20 s and then a final extension at 72°C for 3 min.
Purify the sample using AMPure XP beads: Perform purification in a 2 mL Eppedorf tube Use 1.0x beads Wash 2x with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elude in 12 μL nuclease-free water
Validate the final read-tag libraries on an Agilent 2200 Tapestation with the High Sensitivity D1000 Screentape.
Determine the concentration of the linked-tag library on a Qubit 2.0 fluorometer with the dsDNA HS Assay kit. Use 1 μL sample Perform duplicate measurements
Dilute the read- and linked-tag libraries as follows: %s %s Platform Read-tag libraries Linked-tag library MiSeq 1.3 ng/μL 0.3 ng/μL HiSeq 0.93 ng/μL 0.21 ng/μL
Pool 4.6 μL of each read-tag library with 0.8 μL of the linked-tag library to create the sequencing ready library.
creator: Morten Simonsen Dueholm
